what_it_does: This file is a YAML-frontmatter channel attachment record that links attachment att-12c1d723 to board item item-7eb16874 in the dragnet-development channel, recording only the attachment type and timestamp.
observations: The file contains only YAML frontmatter and no body content
It records an added timestamp in ISO 8601 format with timezone
It belongs to the dragnet-development channel
standards: It is a minimal cross-reference stub with no payload
The target uses a board:// URI scheme
The only signal is the typed link to a board item
conventions: Uses id, channel, attachment_type, target, and added fields
References an external board item with a board:// URI
concerns: No payload or content beyond the linkage
Meaning depends entirely on the external board item
notes: A minimal channel attachment metadata stub that points to a board item in the dragnet-development channel.
questions: What content or artifact is actually attached?
Who created or added this attachment?
What does the target board item represent?
Does board_items indicate a category or an action?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears to be an attachment linkage record and it does exactly that: it records a typed, timestamped link to a board item.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to create a persistent, referenceable association between a channel attachment identifier and a specific board item.
novelty: Use of a board:// URI scheme to target a board item within a channel attachment record.
contribution: Provides a timestamped, typed link from an attachment identifier to a board item, enabling traversal from channel attachments into board item context.
summary_for_embedding: This file is a YAML metadata record for channel attachment att-12c1d723 in the dragnet-development channel, declaring attachment_type board_items and targeting board item item-7eb16874 with an added timestamp.
intention_for_embedding: It was meant to persist a reference linking a channel attachment to a specific board item.
